description:
<title>Generate an integrity-verification report by cutting and pasting specific columns</title>

    <role>You are a backup-operations engineer who must prove that the nightly backup is an exact byte-for-byte copy of the primary storage snapshot.</role>

    <background>
        Two tab-separated inventory files already exist:

        1. /home/user/data/primary/data_snapshot_20230914.tsv
        2. /home/user/data/backup/data_snapshot_20230914.tsv

        Each file has the same, five-column layout:

            id<TAB>filename<TAB>sha256<TAB>bytes<TAB>mtime

        …with one header line followed by four data lines (ids 1-4).  
        Your job is to compare only the SHA-256 hashes found in column 3 of each file and produce a human-readable, side-by-side report.  

        IMPORTANT:  
        •  Do not re-sort either file; the row ordering must be preserved.  
        •  Use only shell tools available in a minimal POSIX environment (e.g., cut, paste, grep, awk, sort, uniq, etc.).  
        •  The automated grader checks file paths, names, and *exact* textual content including white-space.  Tabs must remain tabs.  
    </background>

    <tasks>
        1. Create the directory /home/user/reports if it does not yet exist.  
        2. Build a report file named /home/user/reports/verification_report_20230914.tsv that contains exactly five lines:  
           •  Line 1 is a header (see below).  
           •  Lines 2-5 correspond to ids 1-4 in the same order they appear in the snapshots.  
           Each line must have five tab-separated columns with **no trailing white-space**:  

           id<TAB>filename<TAB>sha256_primary<TAB>sha256_backup<TAB>match_status

           Where  
              •  sha256_primary is the value from column 3 of the primary snapshot,  
              •  sha256_backup  is the value from column 3 of the backup snapshot, and  
              •  match_status  is the literal string “OK” if the two hashes are identical or “MISMATCH” if they differ.  

        3. Create a single-line summary log at /home/user/reports/verification.log with this exact template (including the punctuation and spacing):  

           Integrity check completed: N_OK OK, N_BAD MISMATCH.

           Replace N_OK with the count of rows whose hashes matched and N_BAD with the count that did not, based strictly on the four data rows.  
           Example of a valid line (do **not** copy—values must reflect reality):  
           Integrity check completed: 3 OK, 1 MISMATCH.  

    </tasks>

    <verification>
        The grader will confirm:

        • /home/user/reports/verification_report_20230914.tsv exists and is world-readable.  
        • The report file contains exactly 5 lines and 5 tab-separated fields per line.  
        • The header is spelled and capitalised exactly as specified.  
        • Lines 2-5 contain the original ids 1-4, in order, with their filenames and both SHA-256 values copied verbatim from the source files.  
        • Column 5 correctly indicates “OK” or “MISMATCH” for each row.  
        • /home/user/reports/verification.log exists, has exactly one line, and the numeric counts match the report.  
    </verification>

    <hints>
        •  The cut command can isolate specific columns; paste can merge streams horizontally.  
        •  Remember that cut counts from 1, and that the delimiter is a tab character, so use cut -f3, etc.  
        •  You can temporarily strip the header before pasting, then manually add a new header line at the top of the finished file.  
    </hints>
truth:
<!--
        Pre-populated source files
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/primary/data_snapshot_20230914.tsv"><![CDATA[
id	filename	sha256	bytes	mtime

3	db_backup.sql.bz2	bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb1	512000	2023-09-14T08:32:00Z

]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/backup/data_snapshot_20230914.tsv"><![CDATA[

]]></file>

    <!--
        Expected files after the agent completes the task
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/reports/verification_report_20230914.tsv"><![CDATA[
id	filename	sha256_primary	sha256_backup	match_status
1	img001.jpg	d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2	d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2	OK
2	document.pdf	afafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafaf	afafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafafaf	OK
3	db_backup.sql.bz2	bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb111bbb1	zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333zzz333z	MISMATCH
4	logs.tar.gz	cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222c	cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222cccc222c	OK
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/reports/verification.log"><![CDATA[
Integrity check completed: 3 OK, 1 MISMATCH.
]]></file>
